description_long: This is an electric guitar with a classic single-cutaway body shape, finished in a worn, off-white color with visible signs of use and aging. The body features a black pickguard and two visible pickups, along with a metallic bridge and control plate housing the volume and tone knobs as well as a selector switch. The neck is made of a lighter wood, likely maple, and is fitted with a dark fingerboard and metallic frets. The headstock is elongated and slightly curved, with tuning pegs arranged in a single row along one side, and a small logo or marking near the tip. The guitar's overall design is reminiscent of the iconic Fender Telecaster, with a utilitarian yet stylish appearance. This instrument is suitable for street performances, garage bands, or as an urban decorative element.
description: A worn, off-white electric guitar with a black pickguard, metallic hardware, and a single-cutaway body reminiscent of a Fender Telecaster.
description_view_0: Front view showing the body, black pickguard, pickups, bridge, and the full neck with headstock.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the guitar's thin profile, bridge, and the length of the neck.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the back of the body and neck, with less detail on the pickguard and hardware.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the guitar's profile, neck, and headstock from a different angle.
attribute_headstock_design: Elongated, slightly curved headstock with six inline tuning pegs and a possible logo near the tip
